title: Components, Cycles, Topological Order, and Unweighted Paths · Trace Retrieval
summary: Validate a deployment dependency plan and explain unreachable service routes. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a dependency analyzer with path evidence after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Find connected components and explain what connectivity means for the modeled domain.
Detect undirected and directed cycles with the correct parent or active-path state.
Produce and validate a topological order or return concrete cycle evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Analyze directed edges A→B, A→C, B→D, C→D, and D→B. Report traversal order separately from reachability, return a cycle witness, and reject invalid topological claims.

Inspect the Multi Source Bfs trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

content.text: For Multi Source Bfs, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Running separate BFS from every source has the same cost.
